title: Writer sees need for philosophical thought to address social, political challenges
content: He was speaking after releasing a book, Vartamanada Vivechaneya Dikkugalu (Directions of Contemporary Critical Thought), in Kalaburagi

title: Efforts on to set up interim firefighting facility in Kozhikode city following SHRC order
content: The move comes in the wake of widespread public outcry after the beach fire station was completely shifted out to Meenchanda, leaving the city’s prime commercial hubs without immediate emergency cover

title: Karnataka’s Bidadi Township project and the land acquisition dispute | Explained
content: The Bidadi AI Township project, proposed to alleviate urban congestion, has led to widespread protests from farmers in the region and also reignited political tensions. What makes the project so contentious?

title: Tamil Nadu Cabinet discusses on Budget
content: Law Minister Nirmalkumar slams Opposition over corruption and also alleged that the DMK, AIADMK and BJP are attempting to form a government together.

title: Protest by people displaced by Hidkal Dam continues in Belagavi
content: The day-and-night protest seeking fair compensation and proper rehabilitation for land acquired for the dam enters the third day

title: Minister downplays risks from ‘data breach’ at Kudankulam nuclear plant
content: Speaking to The Hindu, Union MoS Jitendra Singh says the breach has nothing to do with nuclear security at the Kudankulam nuclear power plant; experts also do not seem worried by the incident

title: Kejriwal visits Wangchuk at Jantar Mantar; recalls 2011 anti-corruption movement with Anna Hazare
content: The AAP chief urged Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan to step down, and suggested Sonam Wangchuk be made the country’s education minister

title: Puri Rath Yatra: At least 1 dead, many ill in crowd surge
content: Security personnel and emergency rescue teams quickly intervened to assist those affected by the dense crowding

title: Cyber police issue lookout circular for controversial Kerala YouTuber ‘Thoppi’
content: A case was registered against him on June 20 for allegedly posting obscene visuals through streaming platforms

title: Third language must stop, not start, in Class 9: Justice Nagarathna
content: Court advises new TVK-led Tamil Nadu government to not deny the children of the State the benefit of Central schools through the Navodaya scheme, suggests the State could have its ‘own education system’ without denying children the option of enrolling in Navodaya schools

title: Students of Shivamogga school denied non-veg food for years; KDSS raises concern
content: The school hosts around 250 students from classes 6 to 10, hailing from various areas within the Shivamogga district

title: State Election Commissioners face controversy in Kerala and Tamil Nadu
content: Controversies in Kerala and Tamil Nadu highlight concerns over the integrity and autonomy of the State Election Commission

title: Cyberabad civic body to conduct divine-waste collection drive at five temples on July 18
content: The collected divine waste will be scientifically segregated and processed through authorised recycling and environmentally responsible disposal methods

title: HRD Minister Nara Lokesh says A.P.’s doors are always open for Amara Raja Group
content: The harassment faced by the group during the YSRCP term should never have happened, the Minister says in a social media post, while hoping that the company’s biggest chapters will still be written in the State where its journey began

title: Calicut University plans special academic zone on main campus
content: Vice-Chancellor P. Raveendran dismisses allegations over appointments of pro Vice-Chancellor, and director of press and publications divisions

title: Shiv Sena seeks judicial probe into ‘fake encounter’ in Kalaburagi
content: Siddalinga Swami says it involves police action against a prisoner who was among the three who escaped from the Central Prison

title: NTR District Collector announces contest for best photo-journalists
content: Contest will be held in three categories—A.P. Heritage, Tourism and Culture; Youth, P4, Economic Development and Welfare; and Best News Photograph

title: ‘Varavu’ movie review: A dated revenge film that refuses to serve anything novel
content: Though the typical Shaji Kailas elements appear in ‘Varavu,’ with an added dose of gore, much of it fails to leave a mark due to the lack of emotional underpinnings

title: ‘I, Nobody’ movie review: An unconventional thriller that fumbles in the final act
content: Technically, the film is on par with some of the best works from Malayalam cinema in recent years, with slick, thoughtful framing and editing.

title: Assam’s out-of-school children dream of becoming MLAs, teachers
content: Some of the 45 such children who attended three 10-day motivational camps want to become teachers to impact lives around them

title: ED searches 13 locations in alleged terror-funding and illegal infiltration case
content: The case is linked to a First Information Report (FIR) registered by the Anti-Terror Squad (ATS) of Uttar Pradesh. The searches were carried out in Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, West Bengal, and Maharashtra

title: ED arrests accused in Loni cooperative society case
content: Members of the public were induced to invest in schemes with the promise of high returns, and the invested amounts were misappropriated, the Enforcement Directorate says

title: Supreme Court shifts contempt pleas on ‘bulldozer justice’ to High Courts
content: Each one of the contempt petitions involves disputed facts which would require separate and in-depth enquiries, Chief Justice of India Surya Kant says

title: Vijayendra, Bommai lead protest over deteriorating law and order in State
content: Hundreds of BJP workers take part in the protest march even as the two leaders accuse the State govt. of pursuing appeasement politics

title: How ‘bulldozer justice’ undermines the law
content: The image of swift destruction creates an impression of decisive leadership, but it also normalises the idea that executive authority can override legal safeguards

title: All set for Vallasadya at Aranmula
content: The Vallasadya of the Aranmula Sree Parthasarathy temple in Pathanamthitta, a ritual feast attached to the Onam festivities, is set to begin on July 19. The event includes participation from 51 palliyodams. The annual Aranmula Uthrattathi Boat Race is scheduled for August 30.

title: Residential towers for legislators nearing completion in Amaravati, says MA&UD Minister Narayana
content: They will soon be handed over to the General Administration Department, says the Minister after inspecting the progress of key constructions in the capital region

title: Kerala CPI charters entire train to take party workers to Delhi protest against Union govt.
content: CPI’s national protest with the slogan ‘Delhi Chalo, Badlaav Zaroori Hai,’ against the policies of the Union government, will witness the participation of cadres from across the country

title: Palaniswami urges TVK govt. to execute Nadanthaai Vaazhi Cauvery project immediately
content: It is aimed at rejuvenating the Cauvery river and its tributaries and curbing the mix of of sewage with the river system

title: Kerala govt. order declares Neriamangalam-Valara route as revenue land
content: The order clarifies the land’s status and raises hopes that the road-widening work on the stretch on the Kochi–Dhanushkodi National Highway, which has been stalled since July 2025  following a Kerala High Court order, will resume soon.

title: Congress to raise Ram Temple donation issue, oppose delimitation Bill in Parliament
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge writes to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, urging him to convene an all-party meeting before any revised version of the delimitation Bill is introduced in the monsoon session of Parliament

title: Kirlampudi police book YSRCP leader Ambati Rambabu for disrupting their duties
content: The former Minister and his followers are accused of pushing aside the police during the last rites of Kapu leader Mudragada Padmanabham

title: Sabarimala review: A strict definition of ‘denomination’, ‘essential religious practices’ will compress plural and diverse Hinduism, Centre tells SC
content: Eight years ago, a majority judgment of September 2018 had rejected ‘Ayyappans’ as a religious denomination and dismissed prohibition on women aged between 10 and 50 years from entering the Sabarimala temple as an essential religious practice

title: Five kumkis, 50 personnel capture rogue tusker on Chittoor-Tamil Nadu border
content: The sub-adult elephant, which crossed the Moghili Ghat highway repeatedly, injured a forest officer and once drew a 3,000-strong crowd into Palamaner town, was captured after a day-long operation and shifted to the Musalimadugu camp

title: Mallikarjun Kharge writes to PM Modi, seeks all-party meeting on delimitation
content: Mallikarjun Kharge requested Prime Minister Narendra Modi to provide the Opposition adequate time to study the proposals on delimitation before it is introduced in Parliament

title: Andhra Pradesh’s retail inflation above national average for fifth month running
content: The State’s retail inflation rose to 4.9% in May 2026 against a national 3.9%, with personal care and miscellaneous services seeing the steepest rise; the government has placed inflation on its ‘Needs Attention’ watchlist

title: India’s first privately developed orbital-class rocket Vikram-1 set for July 18 lift-off
content: Private space launch company Skyroot Aerospace on July 16, 2026 announced that it will attempt to launch Vikram-1 from the space port’s first launch pad at 11.30 a.m..

title: 2024 Nellai Express cash seizure case: T.N. BJP president Nainar Nagenthran appears before court
content: In April 2024, ₹3.99 crore was seized from three men aboard Nellai Express, at Tambaram. The cash was allegedly being taken for distribution to voters in Tirunelveli following the instruction of Mr. Nagenthran who was contesting in Lok Sabha election

title: ISRO successfully carries out tests of Gaganyaan crew module systems
content: One of the tests involved separation of the ‘umbilical mechanism’ that serves as a link between the crew module, where astronauts live, and the service module that provides propulsion, says the agency

title: Delimitation Bill should be fair, says Supriya Sule, refutes speculations about joining NDA
content: Along with a 50% increase in seats for all States, there should be clarity about the process as well, says NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule

title: NDA steps up push for numbers ahead of delimitation Bill, Opposition closes ranks
content: Both the ruling alliance and Opposition INDIA bloc are firming up strategies ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament; NDA banks on splits in Trinamool and Sena and the realignment in T.N.; Congress’s parliamentary group is scheduled to meet today

title: T.N. Forest Department to consider permitting visitors to Chadivayal elephant camp in Coimbatore district
content: The plan is to allow visitors to witness feeding of elephants as permitted in Theppakadu elephant camp in the Mudumalai Tiger Reserve and Kozhikamuthi elephant camp in the Anamalai Tiger Reserve

title: Fourth Trinamool Congress MP, actor Koel Mallick, resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: Three former Trinamool Congress Rajya Sabha MPs, Sushmita Dev, Prakash Chik Baraik, and Sukhendu Sekhar Ray, recently filed nomination for their seats in Rajya Sabha as Bharatiya Janta Party candidates.

title: Ground water levels fall in 18 districts; Telangana releases agriculture contingency plan for Vanakalam 2026
content: Majority of the major, medium, and minor irrigation reservoirs in Telangana are presently at, or very close to, their Minimum Draw Down Level (MDDL)

title: Delhi HC directs daily medical monitoring of Sonam Wangchuk amid indefinite hunger strike
content: The petition argued that the government could not remain a passive spectator, contending that failure to intervene could amount to ‘abetment of suicide’ under Section 108 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023

title: 'Make Wangchuk education minister': Kejriwal joins protest, seeks Pradhan's ouster
content: Arvind Kejriwal joined Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. He demanded the removal of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Kejriwal also endorsed Wangchuk as a potential replacement for the minister. He highlighted recurring exam paper leaks and student suicides. The former Delhi CM urged the government to listen to youth concerns.

title: Rajasthan school teacher 'strip-searches' students over missing Rs 500, suspended
content: A teacher in Rajasthan was suspended following serious complaints from villagers. Locals alleged students were strip-searched after money went missing from a classroom. However, officials investigated and found these allegations to be entirely false. Only student bags were checked by other students, not the teacher herself. The department suspended the teacher to protect its image despite the false claims.

title: Europe’s heatwave has an important lesson for smartphone users across the world
content: Europe's severe heatwave causes smartphones to overheat, leading some to place them in fridges. Experts caution this practice creates internal moisture damage and can harm devices. Swollen batteries and non-functional phones are common after this cooling attempt. Users should keep phones out of direct sunlight and avoid intensive apps. Turning off features like Bluetooth and GPS can also help prevent overheating.

title: 'No better person': Ashwin backs MS Dhoni to succeed Fleming as CSK coach
content: Ravichandran Ashwin discussed Chennai Super Kings' new chapter after Stephen Fleming's departure. He believes MS Dhoni would be the ideal choice to lead the team. Ashwin noted the exceptional Fleming-Dhoni partnership that brought significant success. He also highlighted the challenges for any new coach due to Dhoni's influence. The franchise faces a crucial decision shaping its future direction.

title: 'Cabinet expansion very soon': Cong hints at Karnataka reshuffle after Rahul-DKS meeting
content: Karnataka cabinet expansion is expected to occur very soon. Senior Congress leaders met in Delhi to discuss the long-pending cabinet appointments. Consultations are underway to fill twenty vacant ministerial positions and the Speaker's post. The party aims to balance regional and caste equations for the selections. This expansion is anticipated before the upcoming assembly session begins in August.

title: South Bengaluru horror: Man strangles pregnant wife with dog's leash, dies by suicide
content: A man allegedly strangled his pregnant wife to death with a dog's leash. He then died by suicide at their rented house in Banashankari Park. The house owner alerted police after hearing loud arguments and screams. Police discovered the wife's body in the bedroom and the husband hanging. Investigations are ongoing to determine the motive behind the tragic incident.

title: Jeff Bezos donates $10m to LA that city's mayor says is about investing in culture
content: Jeff Bezos' Earth Fund has granted $10.1 million for Watts Towers Arts Center Campus improvements. This investment will expand usable park space and introduce new cooling features. The project aims to strengthen the campus as a cultural hub before the 2028 Olympics. Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass welcomed the significant milestone for the community. This funding advances commitments to green space and climate resilience efforts.

title: From no prom dress to $34M gift: Lauren Sánchez’s fashion donation story
content: In a significant philanthropic move, Lauren Sanchez and Jeff Bezos have pledged $34 million towards cutting-edge sustainable textile research. This funding aims to advance the creation of biodegradable fibers and plastic-free synthetic silk. For Sanchez, this initiative is particularly meaningful, having faced personal hardships in her youth. The couple also toasted their first wedding anniversary aboard their luxurious superyacht, amplifying their commitment to environmental conservation and community support.

title: IBM CEO in letter to investors: What played out was worse than our expectations
content: IBM shares experienced a substantial historical drop this week. This significant decline followed weaker-than-expected second-quarter financial results. Large deals failed to close, and customers shifted spending priorities. IBM CEO Arvind Krishna acknowledged the company faltered in adapting to market changes. Despite challenges, IBM highlighted strengths in software and consulting growth.

title: Indralok Aawasiy Yojana 2 BHK flats: Eligibility, price, registration fee and lottery rules
content: Uttarakhand residents can now own homes through the Indralok Aawasiy Yojana in Haridwar.  This scheme offers two categories of flats for different income groups.  Registration requires a ten percent deposit and a nominal processing fee.  Applications are verified before a transparent lottery selects beneficiaries for these homes.  Successful applicants can pay in full or use an instalment plan.

title: Watch: Argentina fans burn England flags after FIFA WC semi-final win
content: Argentina's World Cup semi-final win over England was overshadowed by controversial celebrations, with some fans burning England flags, displaying St George's Cross coffins and chanting Falklands War songs. Players also posed with a “Las Malvinas son Argentinas” banner after the match. President Javier Milei reiterated Argentina's claim, saying, “Sovereignty is not negotiable”.

title: H-1B returnees face tough Indian IT market with lower pay, fewer jobs
content: Recruitment trends indicate that companies are hiring cautiously, and many returnees are getting jobs at less than their salary expectations. Artificial intelligence has compounded the challenge since many roles have become redundant.

title: Capital of contrasts: While Delhi moves on, Kirari remains under water
content: Kirari residents endure sewage-laden water and illness after recent heavy monsoon rains.  Children cannot attend flooded schools which remain submerged for days.  Public parks have become filthy lakes, and homes are inundated with water.  Locals navigate streets using makeshift pathways, facing chronic civic neglect.  Authorities are constructing drainage systems to resolve the recurring waterlogging issue.

title: India's real estate equity inflows hit record $8.5 billion in H1 2026: CBRE
content: India's real estate sector achieved record equity capital inflows in early 2025. Land and office asset acquisitions fueled this significant growth in capital. Domestic investors showed strong conviction, contributing most of the investment inflows. The momentum is expected to continue through the year. Global capital is anticipated to return as conditions stabilize.

title: 'Those under pressure, go wherever you want': Mamata as TMC exodus continues
content: Mamata Banerjee addressed the Trinamool Congress exodus after the recent assembly poll defeat. She suggested leaders facing pressure are free to leave the party. Rajya Sabha MP Koel Mallick resigned, adding to the growing list of exits. This resignation followed Madan Mitra quitting to join a rebel TMC faction. Banerjee also defended her nephew Abhishek Banerjee amid internal party issues.

title: ICC reprimands Indian cricketer for throwing ball in 'dangerous manner' ahead of 2nd ODI
content: India fast bowler Gurnoor Brar was warned by the ICC and given one demerit point for breaching the Code of Conduct during the first ODI against England after throwing the ball towards Ben Duckett in a manner deemed "inappropriate and dangerous." Despite the incident, Brar took two wickets as India won by six wickets to lead the three-match series 1-0.

title: Low-cost smart munitions and cruise missiles, is this the future of combat?
content: Modern conflicts highlight the need for precision weapons and their scalable deployment. High-end precision munitions prove prohibitively expensive in prolonged engagements. Nations are developing cheaper alternatives to sustain extended combat operations. The US Air Force plans thousands of affordable cruise missiles for future conflicts. This shift prioritizes affordability and rapid production for modern warfare demands.

title: 'Manmohan govt engaged with Anna Hazare ... ': Omar says Centre unmoved by Wangchuk
content: Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah supports Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Wangchuk is protesting alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, demanding accountability. His health has significantly deteriorated after nineteen days of fasting. Doctors warn of serious health complications if the hunger strike continues. The Delhi High Court has ordered daily medical examinations for Wangchuk.

title: Renting a home without a written agreement? Here's why it may be risky
content: In India, verbal rental agreements often lead to misunderstandings, as they lack essential written terms and legal safeguards. This scenario exposes tenants to unpredictable rent increases and abrupt evictions. The absence of documentation complicates security deposits, and property repairs frequently result in disputes. To protect both parties, written contracts are now a requirement under the Model Tenancy Act in states that have adopted it.

title: 'Mirzapur: The Movie' director Gurmmeet Singh says film is aiming for an 'A' certificate
content: Mirzapur The Movie targets an Adults Only certification, aiming to retain its intense identity. Director Gurmmeet Singh believes audiences are receptive to mature theatrical content now. The film condenses the sprawling series while maintaining its core essence and narrative focus. Familiar faces return, but audiences can expect unexpected twists and evolving character dynamics. The highly anticipated Mirzapur The Movie is scheduled for release on September 4, 2026.

title: China charms world, now more popular than US; Xi tops Trump on trust: Report
content: Recent global surveys reveal that China's image is increasingly favorable compared to that of the United States. Under Xi Jinping's leadership, he commands greater trust internationally than Donald Trump does. While personal freedoms remain a strength for the US, this advantage is slowly diminishing as China is perceived to engage less in interventionist policies worldwide. These trends underscore a noteworthy shift in international perceptions.

title: Simone Biles shares ESPYS 2026 BTS with Suni Lee after comeback announcement
content: Simone Biles and Suni Lee grabbed attention both on and off the red carpet at the 2026 ESPYS in New York City. The two Olympic gymnastics stars impressed fans with their stylish outfits, while Biles also shared behind-the-scenes moments on her Instagram Story. Their appearance quickly became one of the biggest talking points from the annual sports awards ceremony.

title: 'Lesson for playing with his life': How secret marriage led to Bengaluru student's murder
content: Two brothers allegedly threatened a woman before she was fatally stabbed at her Bengaluru home.  The victim, Amrutha, died from her injuries two days after the brutal attack occurred.  Police arrested brothers Dhanush and Surya in connection with the violent murder case.  Amrutha ended her relationship with Dhanush after discovering his prior marriage and child.  The dispute between the families reportedly escalated after the breakup, leading to this tragedy.

title: IND vs ENG: Why is KL Rahul not playing in India vs England 2nd ODI at Cardiff?
content: KL Rahul missed India's second ODI against England in Cardiff due to illness, with Ishan Kishan replacing him in the playing XI. Confirming the change at the toss, captain Shubman Gill said, "KL is sick and Ishan comes in for him." England won the toss and chose to bowl, while India lead the three-match series 1-0.

title: Falkland poster row spills over: British govt seeks FIFA action against Argentina
content: Argentina defeated England 2-1 in the FIFA World Cup semifinal on Wednesday in Atlanta. During the post-match celebrations, Argentine players posed with a banner handed to them by supporters in the stands that read, "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" ("The Malvinas are Argentine"). Argentina refers to the Falkland Islands as Islas Malvinas.

title: Marriage certified by a notary? MP HC says they have no such power
content: The Madhya Pradesh high court suspended a notary's license for preparing a fake marriage document. The court ruled that notaries lack authority to function as marriage officers. A young woman was freed after alleging coercion into a sham court marriage. She expressed her desire to return to her parents and continue studies. The court ordered further investigation into the notary's actions.

title: 'Convene an all-party meeting': Kharge writes to PM Modi on revised Delimitation Bill
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to call an all-party meeting. He requested adequate time for opposition parties to study the revised delimitation proposals. The Congress party plans to strongly oppose the proposed delimitation bill if reintroduced. They will also work to maintain unity among all opposition parties. The party supports women's reservation but opposes linking it to delimitation.

title: 'Wanted papers': Drunk son kills father over Rs 150cr property dispute in Ghaziabad
content: A 32-year-old man allegedly shot his father dead after a heated argument. The dispute began when the father confronted his son for returning home drunk. A land dispute was also ongoing between the father and his elder son. The son then fired multiple bullets at his father, killing him instantly. Police have registered a murder case and are searching for the accused.

title: Another setback for TMC, Koel Mallick resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: TMC leader Koel Mallik resigned from the Rajya Sabha on Thursday. This follows Madan Mitra's departure and joining a rebel TMC faction. Earlier, Sukhendu Sekhar Ray and Sushmita Dev also resigned from the party. They subsequently joined the Bharatiya Janata Party and filed nomination papers. The BJP defended its decision to field these former TMC leaders.

title: Justin Gaethje's UFC future: Did his mother decide to end it?
content: Justin Gaethje’s future inside the UFC Octagon has become a hot topic after his stunning victory over Ilia Topuria at Freedom 250. The American shocked the MMA world by handing Topuria his first professional defeat and capturing the undisputed UFC lightweight title in the historic event held at the White House. While fans continue celebrating the memorable win, questions have already shifted toward whether Gaethje will fight again.

title: Messi magic and tactical panic: How England engineered their semi-final downfall
content: Thomas Tuchel’s plan worked until England scored, parked the bus and invited Lionel Messi to spend 37 minutes dismantling it. Read how England engineered its own downfall in today's Offside.

title: Unmanned wingmen: Drones fire air-to-air missiles, rewriting air combat rules
content: The US Air Force's YFQ-44A Fury drone successfully launched its first live AMRAAM missile. This historic test marks the first live missile launch by a US Collaborative Combat Aircraft. Anduril's Lattice software enabled autonomous mission execution and target engagement during the test. Other nations like Turkey and Australia are also advancing similar unmanned combat drone capabilities. These autonomous platforms are increasingly playing a central role in modern warfare scenarios.

title: Ukraine gets new PM: Parliament approves Serhii Koretskyi amid cabinet shuffle
content: Ukraine's parliament approved Serhii Koretskyi as the new prime minister on Thursday. This appointment follows the resignation of former prime minister Yulia Svyrydenko. President Zelenskyy cited Koretskyi's energy sector experience for winter preparations. The government reshuffle includes other senior officials and aims to refresh leadership. Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal as defense minister sparked protests and criticism.

title: Tiny creatures of sea expose big story of Gujarat’s plastic waste
content: Barnacles on Gujarat's coast show high microplastic contamination levels. Shivrajpur beach, a Blue Flag site, recorded the highest concentration of these particles. Researchers found microplastics in barnacles, seawater, and coastal sediments across thirteen locations. Plastic waste entering rivers and industrial effluents contribute significantly to this marine pollution. These findings highlight the widespread presence of plastic pollution in the marine ecosystem.

title: 'Diego is enjoying this immensely from up there': Messi's emotional tribute to Maradona
content: Argentina secured a place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after defeating England. Lionel Messi provided two assists, enabling Argentina to overcome a deficit. He dedicated the significant victory to the legendary Diego Maradona. This win mirrors Maradona's iconic performance against England forty years prior. Argentina will now face Spain in the championship match.

title: 5 Of Family Killed After Scooter Crashes Into Parked Tractor In Chhattisgarh
content: The accident occurred under Kumhari police station limits adjoining Raipur when the five family members were travelling on the scooter.

title: "Everyone Does Naarebaazi In Democracy": Top Court Relief For Bhagwant Man
content: A bench of Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, Justice Joymalya Bagchi and Justice V Mohanawas was hearing the Chandigarh administration's challenge to the Punjab and Haryana High Court order

title: 1 Dead, Several Taken To Hospital After Crowd Surge At Puri Rath Yatra
content: One person died and several others were injured after a crowd surge at the iconic Rath Yatra celebrations in Odisha's Puri on Thursday.

title: 'Operations On Track': OnePlus Junks Report Of Quitting India
content: Junking a report on Thursday, the company told NDTV Profit that OnePlus India continues to operate its business as usual.

title: Mumbai's Taj Hotel Issued Rs 22 Crore Notice Over 26/11 Security Barricades
content: The civic body's action targets the heavy security barricades, bollards, and planters erected around the hotel's perimeter in June 2009 following the 26/11 terror attacks.

title: Karnataka Cabinet Reshuffle Delayed, High-Level Meetings Continue In Delhi
content: Amid the delay, a series of back to back meetings took place in New Delhi on Thursday, involving top Congress leaders from Karnataka and the party high command.

title: 2 Brothers Arrested For Beheading Thane Man, Filming Murder On Phone
content: A probe began after a case was registered at Mumbra police station under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita sections pertaining to murder, destruction of evidence and other offences, police said.

title: Delhi May Lease 'Sheesh Mahal' To Luxury Hotel, Keep It Open For State Events
content: The residence will remain available whenever it is needed for official conferences, functions or meetings. During vacant periods, it could be rented out for private programmes, allowing the government to earn revenue while reducing its maintenance expenditure.

title: IPS Officer, RAW Agent, Armyman: Son Of Ex-Bureaucrat And His Many Avatars
content: Yashvardhan, the son of former Uttarakhand Chief Secretary S Ramaswami, was arrested on Thursday during a routine check on the Mussoorie Road.

title: Man Shoots Woman Dead, Then Dies By Suicide In Delhi Flat
content: Police said they received a PCR call about two people lying dead in a flat, rushed to the spot, and launched an investigation.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Was A Russian Doomsday Plane In Iran Recently? Inside The 'Tu-214PU'
content: A Russian Tu-214PU aircraft took off from Moscow and landed in Tehran at around 10:10 am on July 13. The deployment coincided with escalating US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military infrastructure

title: How A German App Is Turning Shade Into A Lifeline During Heatwaves
content: As Europe reels from extreme heatwaves, a German app is helping people stay cool by mapping shaded walking routes. The app uses sun and shadow data to guide commuters away from heat-exposed streets.

title: "Should Hindus Study In Fear?" BJP Leader Slams 'Kalma' Homework
content: The parents of a Class 2 Hindu student alleged that their six-year-old son returned home Wednesday with a homework diary containing instructions to read the "1st and 2nd Kalma" and "Fateh."

title: Hyderabad Parents Claim School Told Student To Learn 'Kalma' For Homework
content: According to the family, their six-year-old child returned home with a homework diary containing instructions to read the 'Kalma' and 'Fateh'.

title: Husband Dead On Floor, Son Stabbed, Karnataka Doctor 'Scrolling' In Bed
content: Police were alerted by neighbours and entered the Ranka Stello Apartments on Karnataka University Road of Dharwad, which lies 435 km from capital Bengaluru. They found Dr Honannavar with fatal stab wounds to the neck.

title: Punjab Congress Alleges AAP Used Cops To 'Kidnap' Local Leader, Workers Clash
content: The Punjab Congress alleged the trigger for the clash was the AAP's attempt to forcefully take away a Congress municipal councillor with the help of the police

title: Top AI Models Are Less Critical Of Repressive Regimes: Meta Report
content: A study showed AI services were echoing the rules of countries that restrict speech and that bias could creep into services used by an increasing number of users.

title: 'Cowardly Crime Against Children': Iran Slams US Attack Near Cancer Hospital
content: Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said that the attack was "reminiscent of Israel's atrocities against healthcare facilities". He said that the attack caused severe suffering and anxiety to the children who were hospitalised.

title: 4 Arrested For Misbehaving With Delhi Poll Officer During Voter Revision
content: According to Delhi Police, the incident took place on July 5 at Fatak Telian in the Chandni Mahal area.

title: 'Many Come And Go': Science Minister Downplays ISRO Resignations
content: To a question about India's future space missions, the Union minister recalled what the space agency's former chief had remarked

title: Golconda Bonalu 2026 begins with gaity as thousands converge on hill-fort to make offerings to Goddess
content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more . Unlock these with Subscription Products you've access to Additional Subscription Benefits Account Settings Need help with your subscription? July 16, 2026 e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective .

title: From 'chanda chori' to paper leaks: Cong to target Modi govt over key issues in Monsoon Session
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." Read More

title: Scientists scanned alien world for radio signals and heard only silence
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
